pages.text: Thembi and Thulani like to visit the park with Grandpa Nathi. They love their Grandpa Nathi.

blocks.text: Thembi and Thulani like to visit the park with Grandpa Nathi.
They love their Grandpa Nathi.

pages.text: Beep Beep Beep But Grandpa Nathi is very sick in the hospital. His eyes are closed. The machine next to Grandpa Nathi goes beep, beep, beep. “Mommy, why won’t Grandpa Nathi sing with me?” asks Thembi. Mommy says, “Grandpa Nathi can still hear you. His heart is singing with you.”

blocks.text: Beep Beep Beep
But Grandpa Nathi is very sick in the hospital. His eyes are closed.
The machine next to Grandpa Nathi goes beep, beep, beep.
“Mommy, why won’t Grandpa Nathi sing with me?” asks Thembi.
Mommy says, “Grandpa Nathi can still hear you. His heart is singing with you.”

pages.text: At home, Thembi and Thulani are waiting at the window. Mommy and Daddy are taking so long at the hospital. Then Thembi sees Mommy and Daddy walk up the driveway. “They are back!”

blocks.text: At home, Thembi and Thulani are waiting at the window.
Mommy and Daddy are taking so long at the hospital.
Then Thembi sees Mommy and Daddy walk up the driveway.
“They are back!”

pages.text: Mommy and Daddy are very sad. “What’s wrong?” asks Thembi. Her tummy feels sore. Mommy’s voice is very quiet. “Grandpa Nathi was old and sick. The doctors couldn’t make him better.” Her eyes fill with tears, “we won’t be seeing Grandpa Nathi again.”

blocks.text: Mommy and Daddy are very sad.
“What’s wrong?” asks Thembi.
Her tummy feels sore.
Mommy’s voice is very quiet. “Grandpa Nathi was old and sick. The doctors couldn’t make him better.”
Her eyes fill with tears, “we won’t be seeing Grandpa Nathi again.”

pages.text: Thulani is sitting outside under Grandpa Nathi’s favourite tree. He doesn’t feel like playing with Thembi. He wishes Grandpa Nathi could read him a story.

blocks.text: Thulani is sitting outside under Grandpa Nathi’s favourite tree.
He doesn’t feel like playing with Thembi. He wishes Grandpa Nathi could read him a story.

pages.text: Thembi is angry and throws her doll. “Why did Grandpa Nathi have to die!” Mommy pulls Thembi close on her lap. “It’s okay to be angry,” says Mommy. “I miss Grandpa Nathi, too.”

blocks.text: Thembi is angry and throws her doll. “Why did Grandpa Nathi have to die!”
Mommy pulls Thembi close on her lap.
“It’s okay to be angry,” says Mommy. “I miss Grandpa Nathi, too.”

pages.text: Today Mommy, Daddy, Thulani and Thembi are wearing their best clothes to go to church for Grandpa Nathi’s funeral. Everyone is coming to remember Grandpa.

blocks.text: Today Mommy, Daddy, Thulani and Thembi are wearing their best clothes to go to church for Grandpa Nathi’s funeral.
Everyone is coming to remember Grandpa.

pages.text: Mommy holds Thembi’s hand as they sing Grandpa Nathi’s favourite songs. Everybody sings and dances too.

blocks.text: Mommy holds Thembi’s hand as they sing Grandpa Nathi’s favourite songs.
Everybody sings and dances too.

pages.text: Thembi can’t sleep. She calls out to Mommy. “Mommy, I miss Grandpa. My heart is so sore!”

blocks.text: Thembi can’t sleep. She calls out to Mommy.
“Mommy, I miss Grandpa. My heart is so sore!”

pages.text: Thembi and Thulani are eating breakfast when Daddy walks in with a big, big, big cardboard tree. “This is a memory tree,” says Daddy. He sticks the cardboard tree on the wall. Mommy has a box of photos. “Take your favourite pictures of Grandpa Nathi and stick them on the tree.”

blocks.text: Thembi and Thulani are eating breakfast when Daddy walks in with a big, big, big cardboard tree.
“This is a memory tree,” says Daddy. He sticks the cardboard tree on the wall.
Mommy has a box of photos. “Take your favourite pictures of Grandpa Nathi and stick them on the tree.”

pages.text: Together they all stick pictures of Grandpa Nathi onto the tree. They remember the fun times they had. Thembi claps excitedly. “This is my new favourite tree!”

blocks.text: Together they all stick pictures of Grandpa Nathi onto the tree. They remember the fun times they had.
Thembi claps excitedly. “This is my new favourite tree!”

pages.text: Daddy smiles. “Now anytime we want to remember Grandpa Nathi, we can come to our memory tree.” “We will never forget Grandpa Nathi. He will always be in our hearts.”

blocks.text: Daddy smiles. “Now anytime we want to remember Grandpa Nathi, we can come to our memory tree.”
“We will never forget Grandpa Nathi. He will always be in our hearts.”
